title: Sensitivity to Prompt Ordering
concept: The model's predictions can shift dramatically based on the order the exemplars are listed in the prompt, introducing instability.

exercises.prompt: What is an 'exemplar' in the context of prompting?

options.text: An example showing the desired input and output.
A special code to delete the AI.

exercises.explain: A prompt exemplar is a sample input-output pair you give to a language model to teach it how to behave (few-shot prompting).

cards.title: Shuffling the Deck
cards.body: Did you know that simply shuffling the examples in your AI prompt can completely change the answer you get? It's like changing the result of a math problem just by swapping the order of the clues!

cards.title: What is Prompt Ordering Sensitivity?
cards.body: Prompt ordering sensitivity means that a language model's output can change drastically depending on the order of the examples (exemplars) you provide in the prompt. Even if you keep the exact same examples, shuffling their sequence can lead to different predictions.

cards.title: The Recency Effect
cards.body: Imagine showing a toddler pictures of animals: first five dogs, then one cat. If you ask them what animal comes next, they might shout 'Dog!' just because they saw it so many times recently. Language models do the same thing; they have a 'recency bias' where they pay extra attention to whatever you put at the very end of your prompt list.

cards.title: Ordering in Action
cards.body:
If you prompt an AI to classify sentiment:
- Example A: 'Happy!' -> Positive
- Example B: 'Sad.' -> Negative

If you put Example A first, it might label a new neutral sentence as 'Positive'. If you put Example B first, it might label the exact same neutral sentence as 'Negative'. The order alone alters its decision!

cards.title: Myth: Order Doesn't Matter
cards.body: People often think that as long as they provide the correct examples, the order doesn't matter. In reality, language models do not treat all positions equally. They are heavily influenced by which example is shown last or first.

cards.title: Visualizing Order Bias
cards.body: Look at how swapping the sequence of the exact same examples changes the final answer of the model, even when the input query is identical.

left.text: Examples: Positive, then Negative. Result: Negative prediction.

right.text: Examples: Negative, then Positive. Result: Positive prediction.

exercises.prompt: What can happen if you change the sequence of exemplars in a few-shot prompt?

options.text: The model's final answer might change.
The model will delete the prompt.
The model will run much faster.
The model will refuse to answer.

exercises.explain: Because AI models are sensitive to prompt ordering, they are unstable when you shuffle the order of input examples, producing different outputs for the same query.

exercises.prompt: True or False: If you keep the exact same examples in a prompt, the model's output will always remain identical regardless of their order.
exercises.explain: Even with the exact same examples, changing their sequence can cause the model to make different predictions due to ordering sensitivity.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence about prompt instability.
exercises.sentence: A language model's predictions can change just by shuffling the [blank] of the examples.
exercises.explain: Changing the order of examples can lead to unstable AI performance, as the model's predictions are highly sensitive to sequence.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is most likely to give a different answer if you swap the order of the instructions?

options.text: An AI model deciding if a movie review is good or bad.

exercises.explain: AI models are highly sensitive to prompt structure and sequence, meaning they can easily change their output based on prompt ordering. Humans are much better at focusing on the overall context regardless of order.

exercises.prompt: If you take a prompt with three examples and swap the order of the first and last examples, what is a likely result?

options.text: The model will crash and show an error code.
The model might change its decision or output style.
The model automatically alphabeticalizes the prompt.
The model will give the exact same answer but faster.

exercises.explain: AI models are highly sensitive to the order of instructions and examples. Simply swapping the order of two examples in a prompt can alter how the model interprets the task, leading to entirely different responses!
